provisions.description: United States Victims of State Sponsored Terrorism Fund Clarification Act. Makes extensive technical corrections to the Justice for United States Victims of State Sponsored Terrorism Act (34 U.S.C. 20144), including: allowing the Special Master to utilize 5 additional DOJ personnel for 1 year; updating application procedures and deadlines; restructuring payment distribution to split funds 50/50 between non-9/11 and 9/11 related victims on a pro rata basis; imposing per-claim caps of $20,000,000 and aggregate family caps of $35,000,000; establishing third-round payment timeline (90-180 days after enactment); increasing penalty deposit percentage from one-half to seventy-five percent; extending fund termination from 2026 to 2030; capping attorney fees for 9/11 victims at 15 percent; and adding new definitions for non-9/11 related victim, 9/11 related victim, 9/11 dependent, 9/11 family member, 9/11 spouse, and 9/11 victim.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 1701. 
United States Victims of State Sponsored Terrorism Fund Clarification Act
(a) 
Short title
This section may be cited as the ''United States Victims of State Spons
notes: This section has its own short title: 'United States Victims of State Sponsored Terrorism Fund Clarification Act'
Amends 34 U.S.C. 20144 extensively across subsections (b), (c), (d), (e), (f), and (j)
Dollar figures ($20,000,000 and $35,000,000) are per-claim and per-family caps within the fund, not appropriations
Includes rule of construction in subsection (c) protecting 9/11 victim claims
Takes effect on date of enactment per subsection (d)

cross_references.target: Justice for United States Victims of State Sponsored Terrorism Act (34 U.S.C. 20144)
cross_references.description: Extensive amendments to fund distribution, definitions, attorney fee caps, and sunset date

cross_references.target: Section 405 of the Air Transportation Safety and System Stabilization Act (49 U.S.C. 40101 note)
cross_references.description: Referenced for 9/11 victim definitions

provisions.description: Repeals Section 1438 of the FAST Act (Public Law 114-94; 129 Stat. 1432), which contained a rescission, and makes a conforming clerical amendment to the table of contents.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 1702. 
Repeal of rescission

(a) 
In general
Section 1438 of the FAST Act (Public Law 114–94; 129 Stat. 1432) is repealed.
notes: Repealing a rescission effectively restores the previously rescinded budget authority
The FAST Act is the Fixing America's Surface Transportation Act (P.L. 114-94)
Subsection (b) makes a clerical amendment to the FAST Act table of contents

cross_references.target: Section 1438 of the FAST Act (Public Law 114-94; 129 Stat. 1432)
cross_references.description: Repeals rescission provision

cross_references.target: Section 1(b) of the FAST Act (Public Law 114-94; 129 Stat. 1312)
cross_references.description: Clerical amendment to table of contents

provisions.description: Extends the sunset date in Section 102(b)(1) of the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005 (covering roving wiretaps and certain FISA authorities) from December 15, 2019 to March 15, 2020.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 1703. 
Sunsets

(a) Section 102(b)(1) of the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005 (50 U.S.C. 1805 note) is amended by striking ''December 15, 2019''
notes: Subsection (a) of SEC. 1703
Extends PATRIOT Act surveillance authorities by approximately 3 months
50 U.S.C. 1805 note relates to FISA authorities

cross_references.target: Section 102(b)(1) of the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005 (50 U.S.C. 1805 note)
cross_references.description: Extends sunset from December 15, 2019 to March 15, 2020

provisions.description: Extends the sunset date in Section 6001(b)(1) of the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 (the 'lone wolf' provision) from December 15, 2019 to March 15, 2020.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: (b) Section 6001(b)(1) of the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 (50 U.S.C. 1801 note) is amended by striking ''December 15, 2019'' and inserting ''March 15, 2020''.
notes: Subsection (b) of SEC. 1703
Extends the 'lone wolf' FISA provision by approximately 3 months
50 U.S.C. 1801 note relates to the definition of 'agent of a foreign power' for non-U.S. persons

cross_references.target: Section 6001(b)(1) of the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 (50 U.S.C. 1801 note)
cross_references.description: Extends sunset from December 15, 2019 to March 15, 2020

provisions.description: Exempts budgetary effects of Division B from both Statutory PAYGO scorecards maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010.
provisions.title: VIII
provisions.raw_text: The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go
notes: Subsection (a): Statutory PAYGO scorecard exemption for Division B

cross_references.target: Section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
cross_references.description: Exempts Division B effects from statutory PAYGO scorecards

provisions.description: Exempts budgetary effects of Division B from any Senate PAYGO scorecard maintained for purposes of section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress).

provisions.description: Notwithstanding Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines and section 250(c)(8) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, budgetary effects of Division B shall not be estimated for purposes of section 251 of such Act or for purposes of paragraph (4)(C) of section 3 of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010 as being included in an appropriation Act.
provisions.title: VIII
provisions.raw_text: Notwithstanding Rule 3 of the Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines set forth in the joint explanatory statement of the committee of conference accompanying Co
notes: Subsection (c): Prevents classification of Division B costs under discretionary spending caps (section 251) or as appropriations for PAYGO purposes
Two numbered paragraphs: (1) section 251 of BBEDCA and (2) paragraph (4)(C) of section 3 of Statutory PAYGO Act

provisions.description: For the PAYGO annual report issued after adjournment of the first session of the 116th Congress, and for determining whether a sequestration order is necessary, any debit on the 5-year and 10-year scorecards shall be deducted from the scorecard in 2020 and added to the scorecard in 2021.
provisions.title: VIII
provisions.raw_text: For the purposes of the annual report issued pursuant to section 5 of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010 (2 U.S.C. 934) after adjournment of the f
notes: Subsection (d): Shifts PAYGO debit from 2020 to 2021, effectively deferring any sequestration trigger by one year
Applies to both 5-year and 10-year scorecards

cross_references.target: Section 5 of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010 (2 U.S.C. 934)
cross_references.description: PAYGO annual report and sequestration determination

flagged_issues: SEC. 101(3) and SEC. 101(4) amend sections 136 and 138 of P.L. 116-59; account names for these CR substitutions are not determinable from this bill text alone and require reference to the underlying CR
Census anomaly (SEC. 122) amount of $7,284,319,000 is an annualized rate for operations; actual spending during the CR period is prorated
SEC. 146 and SEC. 147 allow higher apportionment rates without specifying dollar amounts; classified as 'other' with llm_classification
SEC. 148 PILT amount of $400,000 is 'in addition to' CR baseline, making it truly supplemental budget authority
Only Division A extracted per user request; Division B (Health Extenders) not included

All mandatory spending extensions in Title I replace amounts previously set by P.L. 116-59 (the initial CR) with larger amounts reflecting the extended period through December 20, 2019 instead of November 21, 2019.
No new discretionary budget authority in this title; all provisions are mandatory spending amendments to authorizing statutes.
Both SEC. 1201 and SEC. 1202 extend programs from November 21, 2019 to December 20, 2019, increasing the pro-rated amounts from $10,684,931 to $16,643,836 each
All three provisions are date extensions with no explicit dollar amounts; they extend Medicaid-related provisions by approximately 29 days aligned with a continuing resolution expiration
All provisions in Title IV are mandatory spending extensions amending existing law, not discretionary appropriations
SEC. 1401 replaces a prior amount ($1,069,000 through Nov 21) with a new amount ($1,665,000 through Dec 20) — net increase of $596,000 and 29-day extension
SEC. 1402 subsections (a)-(d) each add incremental funding for Nov 22 - Dec 20, 2019 extension period totaling $2,980,000
Both SEC. 1501 and SEC. 1502 appropriate 'such sums as may be necessary' — indefinite amounts not included in total_budget_authority
SEC. 1602 reduces Medicaid Improvement Fund by $427,000,000 (from $2,387,000,000 to $1,960,000,000), likely serving as a budgetary offset
SEC. 1701 contains dollar figures ($20,000,000 and $35,000,000) that are per-claim caps, not appropriations
SEC. 1702 repeals a rescission from the FAST Act (P.L. 114-94, Section 1438), effectively restoring previously rescinded budget authority — amount not specified in this text
SEC. 1703 extends PATRIOT Act and Intelligence Reform Act surveillance authority sunsets by 3 months (Dec 15, 2019 → Mar 15, 2020)
SEC. 1801 contains only budget scoring exemptions and PAYGO adjustments for Division B — no dollar amounts or spending provisions
Subsection (d) defers potential PAYGO sequestration debit from 2020 to 2021
